Adding white to a pure color

quizzes.title: HORT 235: Chain of Distribution
quizzes.description: The 6 traditional stops in the flower distribution chain and their roles.

data.term: 6 stops in the chain of distribution (in order)
data.answer: Cultivator/Grower → Auction → Broker → Wholesaler/Warehouse → Florist/Retailer → Consumer
hints: Farm to table, but for flowers
Starts with growing, ends with buying

data.answer: Plant, nurture, harvest when mature, sort, label, and package flowers
Agricultural products are bought and sold; flowers packaged and shipped by air and truck
hints: Like a farmers market on steroids

data.answer: Coordinate shipping and customs; receive large quantities, sell and ship by air/truck
Source from global cultivators, sell to florists/retailers/customers; bridge the gap; ensure freshness; market intelligence and pricing
Design and deliver flowers to end customer; artistic talent; cater to weddings, events, walk-ins; condition flowers
Receive flowers as gifts; end point for flowers; the final goal
hints: The person who gets the bouquet

Attaches a metal pick to the stem

data.answer: Lengthen stem (use tape to give texture)
hints: Wrap with tape for a natural look

Sticks to the bottom of container
Goes across the top of foam
Grid pattern to hold foam down

data.answer: Air-drying, Drying by burying, Pressing, Treating with Glycerin, Freeze-drying
hints: Some use air, some use chemicals

data.answer: Something that sucks all the moisture out
Never wilt, can rearrange, store for later, prep ahead, no allergies, easily manipulated, any color, less expensive long-term
Can fade/collect dust/become outdated, storage problems, lack fragrance, need maintenance/cleaning
